skill.name: version-planner
skill.description: Breaks product ideas and requirements into staged version plans from MVP to V1.0.
skill.summary: Create practical MVP and release roadmaps from product requirements.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a weak-crypto heuristic, and high entropy in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by fenced examples, inline Markdown, frontmatter text, and Chinese-language content. No prompt injection, code execution, network access, secret access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backticks at SKILL.md line 60 start a Markdown template example, not Ruby or shell execution. The surrounding content describes the expected output format for a version plan.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown fence marker and contains no executable command or interpreter context.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fence Close
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backticks at SKILL.md line 95 close the Markdown example opened earlier. They do not invoke a shell, Ruby runtime, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is only a closing Markdown fence in documentation, so there is no execution path.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Inline Filename Template
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 127 uses inline Markdown backticks around a suggested filename pattern. It is documentation for where to save the generated plan.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap a filename placeholder, not a command string or dynamic execution expression.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Conversation Example Fence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backticks at SKILL.md line 133 start a plain conversation example. The block contains sample dialogue and no executable instructions for shell access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a Markdown sample dialogue block with no code execution mechanism.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Heuristic
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md line 3 appears to match frontmatter description text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or security function is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited line shows only a natural-language skill description and no crypto-related implementation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: High Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high entropy alert at SKILL.md line 1 is not supported by the file content. The file is readable Markdown with Chinese text, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is plain Markdown; the entropy signal is likely caused by non-ASCII language content.

content.value_statement: Product ideas often become too broad before a first release is clear. This skill turns requirements into staged MVP, iteration, and V1.0 plans with validation points.
actual_capabilities: Asks clarifying questions about users, pain points, required features, and constraints.
Separates must-have features from later enhancements.
Creates a staged roadmap from V0.1 MVP through V1.0.
Defines validation points and version boundaries for each release.
Lists open questions and next actions for implementation planning.
limitations: It does not validate market demand or customer willingness to pay.
It does not implement the planned product features.
Time estimates are planning references and need human review.
Technical architecture details may need separate research.

use_cases.title: Shape an Early Product Idea
use_cases.description: Convert a rough idea into an MVP plan with clear scope and next steps.

use_cases.title: Split a Feature Backlog
use_cases.description: Group requested features into practical releases with validation goals.

use_cases.title: Prepare Build Phases
use_cases.description: Turn project requirements into implementation phases before coding starts.

prompt_templates.title: Plan My MVP
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a rough idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan versions for this product idea: [describe idea]. Start with questions, then define a V0.1 MVP.

prompt_templates.title: Break Down Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Organize a list of known features
prompt_templates.prompt: I have these requirements: [list features]. Split them into V0.1, V0.2, V0.5, and V1.0 with clear boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: This first version is too large: [describe scope]. Identify the smallest useful MVP and move other items to later versions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Full Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a version roadmap for [product]. Include target users, core value, dependencies, validation points, open questions, and next actions.

output_examples.input: I want to build a personal knowledge base with notes, tags, sync, sharing, and AI search.
output_examples.output: The plan starts with a V0.1 local note capture flow, then adds editing, backup, sync, sharing, and AI search in later releases.

output_examples.input: Our team wants a client portal with messaging, files, billing, reports, and admin controls.
output_examples.output: The roadmap separates secure login and file exchange as the MVP, then schedules messaging, billing, reporting, and admin tools as distinct iterations.

output_examples.input: I have many feature ideas and do not know what to build first.
output_examples.output: The output identifies the core pain point, ranks features by value and dependency, and proposes a first version that can be validated quickly.

best_practices: Provide the target user, main pain point, and must-have features before planning.
Review the proposed MVP boundary before accepting later-version details.
Use the open questions section to guide technical and product research.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to include every feature in V0.1.
Do not treat effort estimates as commitments without team review.
Do not skip clarification when the product idea is vague.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a staged version plan with product overview, requirements, releases, validation points, open questions, and next actions.

faq.question: Can it plan an MVP?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses V0.1 on the smallest useful version that can validate the core value.

faq.question: Does it write product code?
faq.answer: No. It creates planning guidance and can prepare a roadmap before implementation.

faq.question: Can it handle vague ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks clarifying questions before creating a roadmap when key details are missing.

faq.question: Does it support technical constraints?
faq.answer: Yes. It records known constraints and marks uncertain technical details for later confirmation.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Founders, product managers, and developers can use it to divide product work into practical release stages.
